
# Design of purlins

![Lysaght capacity, double-lapped span, 1 bridging](images/Z20019_cap.png){width="90%" #fig-lysaght-capacity}

Cpi = 0.0 for most positive pressure, refer @sec-internal-pressure-positive

```{julia}
Cpe = fCpe_roof(bldg_yy, x=0m, isUplift=true)
```



## Where kl=1.0
```{julia}
spacing = 1050mm;
loadwidth = spacing
kl = 1.0
puw0 = kl * Cpe * wp_roof_uls.pressure * loadwidth |> kN / m
```

## where area is RA1 or RA3


```{julia} 
loadwidth = spacing
span = 5.6m
kl = fKl(a=bldg_yy.a_roofs, loadwidth=loadwidth, span=span, symbol=:RA1)
```

```{julia}
puw1 = kl * Cpe * wp_roof_uls.pressure * loadwidth |> kN / m
```

## where area is RA2 or RA4

```{julia}  
kl = fKl(a=bldg_yy.a_roofs, loadwidth=loadwidth, span=span, symbol=:RA2)
```

```{julia}
puw2 = kl * Cpe * wp_roof_uls.pressure * loadwidth |> kN / m
```

```{julia}
puw0, puw1, puw2
```

From @fig-lysaght-capacity, capacity is 4.29kN/m > load, pass.

## where area is RC1

```{julia} 
span = 3.9m
kl = fKl(a=bldg_yy.a_roofs, loadwidth=loadwidth, span=span, symbol=:RC1)
```

```{julia}
puw3 = kl * Cpe * wp_roof_uls.pressure * loadwidth |> kN / m
```

From @fig-lysaght-capacity, capacity is 10.18kN/m > load, pass.


purlin summary:

- Use Z20019 double-lapped-span @ 1200mm, 1 bridging


# Design of girts

## single span, grid 1, westward direction (plant north orientation)

for most negative pressure, refer @sec-internal-pressure-positive

```{julia}
Cpi = -0.3
```

```{julia}
Cpe = 0.7 - Cpi
```

### Where kl=1.0

```{julia}  
loadwidth = 1.0m
span = 6.3m
kl = 1.0
puw0 = kl * Cpe * wp_roof_uls.pressure * loadwidth |> kN / m
```

### where area WA1

```{julia}    
kl = fKl(a=bldg_xx.a_walls, loadwidth=500mm, span=span, symbol=:WA1)
```

```{julia}
puw1 = kl * Cpe * wp_roof_uls.pressure * 500mm |> kN / m
```

![](images/Z20019_cap_singlespan.png) {width="90%"}

- use single span Z20019, 1000mm spacing,  1 bridging
